title: AMBROSIA BEETLE SURFACE STERILIZATION
author: M. Elizabeth Moore
materials: Stage beetles in container while preparing for wash.
The tea strainer can comfortably hold 50-100 beetles.

steps.title: Prepare/add 75% Ethanol, 2% bleach, 0
steps.instruction: Prepare/add 75% Ethanol, 2% bleach, 0.01% tween in beakers and add diH2O to two beakers.

steps.title: Use a funnel to add beetles...
steps.instruction: Use a funnel to add beetles to a falcon tube, tapping as necessary to prevent escapees.

steps.title: Remove funnel from tube and cover...
steps.instruction: Remove funnel from tube and cover the top of the falcon tube with one side of the tea strainer.

steps.title: Flip the tube, keep the end...
steps.instruction: Flip the tube, keep the end covered with the strainer, and tap the beetles out into the strainer.

steps.title: Close the tea strainer and use...
steps.instruction: Close the tea strainer and use a small clip to seal any gaps beetles could get out through.

steps.title: Repeatedly dunk the tea strainer with...
steps.instruction: Repeatedly dunk the tea strainer with beetles inside into the beakers for about ten seconds:

steps.title: Dab the stainer to dry on a paper towel
steps.instruction: Dab the stainer to dry on a paper towel

steps.title: Dump beetles into sterile petri dish...
steps.instruction: Dump beetles into sterile petri dish with sterile filter paper to dry.

steps.title: Be careful not to crush beetles...
steps.instruction: Be careful not to crush beetles in the strainer while removing them, use soft forceps to remove any beetles remaining in the strainer. Letting the strainer close may crush beetles and beetles may become stuck in water droplets within the strainer.
